title: Gaming UA Spend Hits $25B in 2025 as AI Fuels 25-40% Creative Growth
summary: Global gaming advertisers spent $25B on UA in 2025 (half in US), per AppsFlyer 2026 report. Top spenders up 25-30% YoY in content, smaller ones 40%. AI lowers costs for scaled ad testing. 45.4% AI use for performance analysis.

title: 2026 Prediction: 50% Mobile Game Creatives AI-Generated
summary: By end-2026, AI-generated creatives will be 50% of mobile gaming marketing assets, matching human quality. Boosts speed, customizability, cost savings. Playables dominant with genre templates like 3x3 puzzles.

title: Top Gaming Advertisers Produce 2,400-2,600 AI Creatives Quarterly, Up 30% YoY
summary: Top advertisers output 2,400-2,600 creatives/quarter in 2026, +30% YoY due to gen AI. UA spend $25B in 2025 chases static audience. Unity's Felix Thé: AI shifts to precision targeting for retention.

title: Generative AI Transforms UA Creatives and Testing in Mobile Games 2026
summary: Gen AI enables dozens of ad variations quickly for testing, shortening refresh cycles for stable performance. Used in contextual targeting post-ATT. Reshaping mobile UA strategy beyond development.

title: 50% Mobile Game Ad Creatives AI-Generated by 2026: 10 Reasons from 2.5 Gamers
summary: 50% of mobile game ad creatives AI-generated by 2026. Jakub from 2.5 Gamers lists 10 reasons to adopt AI workflows now for efficiency in gaming UA.

title: Super Bowl LX 2026: Dozen+ Brands Use AI for Ads, Including Svedka & Silverside AI
summary: Super Bowl 2026 featured 12+ AI ads: Svedka's first primarily AI spot with Silverside AI (post-2025 Coke controversy); Artlist.io full spot in 5 days; Xfinity AI de-aging; Ro pre-prod. 50%+ spots used gen AI.

title: 2026 Gaming Marketing: AI Enables Solo Teams for High-Quality Game Ads
summary: AI revolutionizes gaming marketing in 2026; solo/small teams generate high-quality ads, scaling production for competitive UA in iGaming.
